reasoning: The null-dereference bug in pemToCert() is real at the code level (line 15 of utils.js accesses cert.length without a null guard when exec() returns null on no match), but the finding's claimed impact — a process crash / DoS — is incorrect. The sole call site of pemToCert() in xmlenc.js is on line 29, which sits inside a try { ... } catch (e) { callback(e); } block (lines 19-38 of encryptKeyInfoWithScheme). Any TypeError thrown by cert.length on null is caught by that handler and forwarded to the callback as a normal error. No uncaught exception reaches the process, so no DoS is possible. The reachability claim ('crashes the process') is therefore false; this is at most an ungraceful error path, not a vulnerability.
